Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No direct relation to cheating or fairness
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: No direct relation to betrayal or loyalty
Subversion/ Authority_expl: No direct relation to subversion or authority
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Joke degrades religious group's intelligence
Oppression/ Liberty_expl: Joke limits religious group's freedom from prejudice
moral_expl: Scores close to 0 for most norms, but negative for Harm/Help and Degradation/Sanctity.

scenario: Person X yelled at an elderly person for walking too slowly and being in the way.
Harm/ Help_expl: Person X caused emotional distress
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No direct relation to this norm
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: No direct relation to this norm
Subversion/ Authority_expl: Person X disrespected the elderly person's autonomy
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: Person X showed disregard for the elderly person's dignity
Oppression/ Liberty_expl: Person X restricted the elderly person's freedom of movement
moral_expl: High harm and significant violations of sanctity

scenario: You see an American telling foreigners that the US is an evil force in the world.
Harm/ Help_expl: Criticizing one's own country can be seen as harm
Cheating/ Fairness_expl: No direct relation to cheating or fairness
Betrayal/ Loyalty_expl: Criticizing one's own country can be seen as disloyal
Subversion/ Authority_expl: Questioning the actions of one's country can be seen as subversive
Degradation/ Sanctity_expl: No direct relation to degradation or sanctity
Oppression/ Liberty_expl: Criticizing actions perceived as oppressive can be seen as promoting liberty
moral_expl: Harm is moderately mitigated, but cheating and degradation are absent.
